title: Picrasma tetramera
taxonomy: Picrasma tetramera is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Simaroubaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant belongs to the genus Picrasma, which includes several species of trees and shrubs. Picrasma tetramera is a relatively rare species within this genus, and more research is needed to understand its specific characteristics. The classification of Picrasma tetramera reflects its unique position within the Simaroubaceae family.
uses: Picrasma tetramera, a plant species found in Madagascar, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential therapeutic properties. Its medicinal uses include treating various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems. The plant's extracts have also been reported to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating wounds and skin conditions.
